Located in one of Central Florida’s fastest-growing recreational corridors, this 12-acre RV park presents a rare opportunity for a buyer looking to develop, expand, and capitalize on the booming RV and outdoor hospitality market.​ With flexible zoning already in place—Recreational Vehicle Park (P-RV) and General Agriculture (A-1)—the site is primed for vertical and horizontal growth.​ Whether you’re looking to build out a modern RV resort, introduce park model homes, or add income-generating amenities, the groundwork has already been laid.​

Positioned just outside the entrance to Ocala National Forest and a short drive from Silver Springs State Park, the location is a magnet for outdoor travelers, nature lovers, and long-term snowbird residents alike.​ The current infrastructure includes on-site water, septic, and electrical access, offering a solid base for development with minimized upfront utility investment.​

For experienced developers, operators, or investors entering Florida’s high-demand outdoor living space, this is a strategic opportunity to take advantage of rising land values, increased RV adoption, and the regional shift toward experiential travel.​

Serious inquiries only.​ This opportunity is ideal for visionary developers or investors looking to enter or expand in Florida’s high-growth outdoor lodging sector.​

Key Highlights

  • ​

    Zoning Flexibility for Multi-Use Concepts: Dual zoning (P-RV and A-1) allows a broad range of permitted uses—from additional RV pads and mobile homes to support structures like laundromats, retail kiosks, or recreational facilities.​ This offers the flexibility to adapt the property to changing market needs.​

  • ​

    Established Utility Framework: With septic and well systems already functioning, and access to power available, the park is operationally viable from day one.​ Developers can move quickly to add capacity without major infrastructure delays.​

  • ​

    Operational Track Record: High current occupancy among both short-term guests and long-term residents demonstrates clear demand and validates the site’s performance within the region’s RV and tourism ecosystem.​

  • ​

    Strategic Parcel Configuration: The flat, accessible layout of the land simplifies planning, engineering, and construction—whether expanding RV pads, adding communal areas, or integrating alternative lodging types like tiny homes or cabins.​

Location Highlights

  • ​

    Proximity to Iconic Nature Destinations: Located just minutes from Ocala National Forest, Silver Springs, Lake Weir, and dozens of local springs and trails, the site serves as a natural launchpad for ecotourism and year-round outdoor recreation.​

  • ​

    Positioned for Traveler Convenience: Situated directly off S HWY 314A, the site offers seamless access for RV travelers arriving from Orlando, Gainesville, or The Villages—making it easy to attract both weekend visitors and long-haul tourists.​

  • ​

    Lifestyle and Weather Appeal: Marion County’s warm climate and scenic surroundings appeal to snowbirds, retirees, and digital nomads seeking extended stays during winter and shoulder seasons, creating steady occupancy across the year.​

  • ​

    Limited Nearby Competition: The immediate area lacks modern, amenitized RV resorts—presenting a competitive advantage for developers aiming to elevate offerings and capture underserved market segments.​

details.investmentHighlights:
There is a positive and growing appetite to acquire mobile home parks in Okeechobee, Florida, driven by several compelling market dynamics:

Affordable Housing Demand

Mobile home parks offer a low-barrier entry to homeownership or rental, making them increasingly attractive in markets like Okeechobee where affordable housing options are limited.

Rising housing costs in South and Central Florida are pushing investors to target tertiary markets like Okeechobee for yield and long-term stability.

Interest in Stable, Cash-Flowing Assets

Mobile home parks are viewed as recession-resistant with low turnover and high occupancy rates.

Institutional and private investors alike are drawn to the high cap rates typically found in smaller Florida markets compared to metro areas.

Local Market Appeal

Okeechobee has a growing retiree and seasonal population, ideal for mobile home living.

The city benefits from proximity to Lake Okeechobee and recreation areas, making it attractive for both permanent residents and snowbirds.

Low Operational Costs

Compared to multifamily, MHPs often have lower management and maintenance overhead, especially when tenant-owned homes are prevalent.

This appeals to value-add investors looking to optimize operational efficiencies.

Zoning and Regulatory Climate

Okeechobee County has historically been more permissive toward mobile home developments than many coastal areas

This relative flexibility creates opportunities for expansion or infill development.

Investor Interest:

High Yield Potential: Compared to traditional multifamily assets, mobile home parks (MHPs) often offer superior cap rates, with many investors targeting higher cap rate in tertiary markets like Okeechobee.

Value-Add Opportunities:

Pad Upgrades & Utility Infrastructure: Investors see value in increasing lot counts, upgrading septic and water systems, and billing utilities back to tenants. Thus is the case for the portfolio offered.

details.investmentHighlights:
There is a positive and growing appetite to acquire mobile home parks in Okeechobee, Florida, driven by several compelling market dynamics:

  1. ​

    Affordable Housing Demand; Mobile home parks offer a low-barrier entry to homeownership or rental, making them increasingly attractive in markets like Okeechobee where affordable housing options are limited.

  2. ​

    **Rising housing costs in South and Central Florida are pushing investors to target tertiary markets like Okeechobee for yield and long-term stability. Interest in Stable, Cash-Flowing Assets; Mobile home parks are viewed as recession-resistant with low turnover and high occupancy rates. Institutional and private investors alike are drawn to the high cap rates typically found in smaller Florida markets compared to metro areas.

  3. ​

    **Local Market Appeal; Okeechobee has a growing retiree and seasonal population, ideal for mobile home living. The city benefits from proximity to Lake Okeechobee and recreation areas, making it attractive for both permanent residents and snowbirds.

  4. ​

    **Low Operational Costs; Compared to multifamily, MHPs often have lower management and maintenance overhead, especially when tenant-owned homes are prevalent.

  5. ​

    This appeals to value-add investors looking to optimize operational efficiencies.

  6. ​

    **Investor Interest: High Yield Potential: Compared to traditional multifamily assets, mobile home parks (MHPs) often offer superior cap rates, with many investors targeting higher cap rate in tertiary markets like Okeechobee.

  7. ​

    **Value-Add Opportunities: Pad Upgrades & Utility Infrastructure: Investors see value in increasing lot counts, upgrading septic and water systems, and billing utilities back to tenants. Thus is the case for the portfolio offered.
details.marketingDescription:
Media Realty & Advisors has been appointed exclusive agent for the disposition of four mobile home parks in Okeechobee, FL.​ The parks are located in close proximity to one another with two parks, Dew Drop and Okeechobee Lake, being contiguous.​ Combined these parks represent 175 pads.​

Ownership has recently experienced a shift in motivation and has aggressively reduced the asking price, creating a compelling entry point for investors seeking immediate cash flow with future upside in a supply-constrained submarket.​ All offer will be entertained.​

Highlights of the individual parks

  • ​

    Cloverport Mobile Home Park; 7650 Highway 78 W.​ Okeechobee is located minutes from Lake Okeechobee, Kissimmee River and downtown Okeechobee (2.7 miles) with its dining and shopping, & less than 6 miles from the up-and-coming Bass Pro Resort at 10430 W Highway 78.​ The park contains 27 lot/pads and one CBS studio apartment with laundry facilities rented on an annual basis.​ It sits on over 2.5 acres, with three entrances, sub-metered public water & septic.​ 5 units are park owned new single wide mobile homes, 16 units are tenants owned (lot rent only), and there are 6 vacant pads.​ Water expense is passed through to the tenant's owner mobile homes.​ There are 5 brand 560 SF new mobile one bedroom/one bath homes recently delivered and installed, estimated to rent for $1200 per month.​ The value-add lies in leasing the 6 vacant pads or installing new MH's at a cost of approx.​ $58,000 per unit providing a 9.5%-10% cap rate with an investment estimated at $348,000.​

  • ​

    Dew Drop Mobile Home Park; 8791 Highway 78 W.​, Okeechobee sits on 3.9 acres and contains 57 pads with one folio.​ The park consists of lot rents only as each mobile home is tenant owned.​ The MHP is less than 5 miles to the central Business district of downtown Okeechobee

  • ​

    Okeechobee Lake RV Park; 8951 Highway 78 W.​, Okeechobee contains 66 pads (lot rent only).​ This park is contiguous to Dew Drop Mobile Home Park and together they have a combined 126 pads.​ The park consists of lot rents only as each mobile home is tenant owned.​ The MHP is less than 5 miles to the central Business district of downtown Okeechobee.​ The park has one folio number.​

  • ​

    Palm Drive Trailer Park; 3307 and 3611 SE 36th Ave; Okeechobee The property possesses two folio numbers and is situated on a canal with access to Lake Okeechobee.​ The park has 24 pads and collects rent for lot rent only.​

Investment Overview

Bear Creek is a 9-pad hybrid manufactured housing and RV community located along Trouble Creek Road in New Port Richey, Florida.​ The property includes four fully renovated park-owned mobile homes and five RV pads, providing a mix of stable recurring income and seasonal flexibility.​ With strong in-place rents and recent capital improvements, the park delivers reliable cash flow with minimal near-term maintenance needs.​ The property generates an NOI of approximately $55,000, which reflects a 10.25 percent cap rate at the asking price of $539,000.​ All four mobile homes were renovated between 2023 and 2025 with new kitchens, bathrooms, flooring, electrical systems, HVAC units, and other updates.​ This significantly reduces operating risk and enhances tenant retention.​ The park offers meaningful upside through increasing RV occupancy, optimizing monthly RV rates, and implementing modest rent growth on the mobile homes.​ A pro forma stabilization scenario supports an estimated NOI of roughly $79,000, which places the long-term value of the park in the $880,000 to $1,060,000 range depending on assumed cap rates.​ Bear Creek benefits from a strong local market with persistent demand for affordable housing, steady employment bases, and consistent seasonal RV traffic.​ Small hybrid MH/RV parks in Pasco County are scarce, and comparable assets trade at higher per-pad values.​ Overall, Bear Creek is a turnkey investment with strong in-place cash flow, recently upgraded homes, and clear value-add potential.​ It is well suited for both first-time park investors and experienced operators seeking a small, manageable property with upside.​

details.marketingDescription:
The Esterson MHC Team is pleased to present Chaffee Creek MHP, a stabilized 12-unit manufactured housing community located in Jacksonville, FL.​ This investment offers in-place cash flow with immediate upside through market rent adjustments and infill of vacant lots.​ 

Jacksonville is the most populous city in Florida and one of the fastest-growing metros in the Southeast.​ The market benefits from a diverse economy anchored by logistics, finance, healthcare, and the Port of Jacksonville, along with a strong military presence.​ Population growth and business-friendly economic conditions continue to fuel demand for affordable housing.​ With median three-bedroom apartment rents well above $1,800 per month in the Jacksonville MSA, manufactured housing remains an attractive, lower-cost alternative for families and workforce residents.​ 

Chaffee Creek MHP consists of 12 total units, including 8 tenant-owned homes (TOH), 1 rented single-family home (SFH) at $1,500 per month, 1 vacant park-owned home (POH) underwritten at $650 per month, and 2 vacant mobile home lots underwritten at $700 per month.​ Current average TOH lot rents are $568.75 per month, with upside potential through lease-up of the vacant POH and infill of the two vacant pads at market rates.​ 

The park is serviced by public water (billed back to tenants), private sewer (1:1 septic, included in rent), and curbside trash (direct billed).​ Electric and gas are direct billed to tenants.​ The landlord maintains the common areas, resulting in a simple operating structure with low expense burden.​ Roads are gravel, infrastructure is stable, and the park is not located in a flood zone.​ 

This opportunity is being offered at $750,000, with pro forma financials supporting a stabilized NOI of approximately $78,822 and a 10.5% cap rate on market rents.​ Minimal capital expenditures are anticipated, estimated at $1,800 per lot (~$21,600 total).​ The park’s straightforward utility setup, low management intensity, and upside through rent growth and infill make it an ideal investment for buyers seeking stable cash flow and value-add potential in a top-performing Florida market.​ 

All offers must include purchase price, inspection timelines, appropriate terms, proof of funds, and relevant real estate experience.​
